top_reviews.review_headline: The Last Portable Charger you will ever Need or Want
top_reviews.review_body: I've been through so many portable chargers. They are either huge, get super hot, or charge so slowly!This product shocks me every time I use it. My phone can be on 15%, I plug it up, and within 10mins, give or take, my phone is at 100%. This is one of the best devices I've bought. Its small, but strong. I love the paw print that shows its charging with a blue light. Its compatible with anything, & you can charge more than one thing. It has 2 type C ports, & 1 type A port.You have to get this compatible, small, quick-charging, mighty battery that fits in the palm of your hand.Wonderful gift for the hard-to-shop family member or friend.

top_reviews.review_headline: This Model Proved Itself a Great Buy
top_reviews.review_body: Travel videos review a dizzying and confusing array of chargers, mainly geared towards people with fancy tech. That’s not me. After resisting the idea for a long time, I decided I needed one for an upcoming trip to the Middle East, with long days, many photo opportunities and no electrical outlets within easy reach. I searched for at least 10KmAh, 30W+ based on what would need to be charged at once on the go (1 iPhone and possibly 1 Nikon P950). I settled on this one: It was one of the smallest and lightest one I found; it had the right number and configuration of ports and came with its own cleverly attached cable.The materials feel and look quality. It recharges quickly (about 20-30 min). It charged my iPhone from 20% to 80% in less than 10 min. It is one of the most compact I found at a decent price. Overall, it performed as expected and proved itself a good addition to my travel gear, especially for its price.If you don’t have complicated gear and want to keep it simple and affordable, this brand, and especially this model, is a great buy.

top_reviews.review_headline: Slim power bank that’s easy to carry
top_reviews.review_body: This portable charger is a great option if you want something powerful but still easy to carry around. The 10,000mAh capacity gives plenty of extra power for a phone, and the charger itself is surprisingly slim and lightweight. It fits easily in a bag or even a larger pocket.Charging speeds are good and it works well for keeping devices topped up throughout the day. I also like the simple design and the battery indicator, which makes it easy to see how much charge is left.Overall, it’s a reliable portable charger that’s convenient for travel, commuting, or everyday use. If you’re looking for a slim power bank with solid capacity, this is a really good choice. 🔋

top_reviews.review_headline: Great compact power bank with a built-in cable
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve used quite a few portable chargers over the years, and this one quickly became one of my favorites because of how convenient it is.The 10,000 mAh capacity is a great balance between size and power. It’s small enough to carry in a bag or pocket, but still has enough capacity to recharge my phone more than once.My favorite feature is the built-in USB-C cable. It’s attached to the power bank and doubles as a carrying strap, so I don’t have to remember to pack a separate charging cable. The cable is braided and feels durable, not flimsy like some built-in cables I’ve seen on other chargers.Another nice bonus is the built-in flashlight. It’s a small feature, but it’s actually very handy for emergencies, power outages, or when you just need a quick light while looking for something in a bag.The power bank itself feels solid and well made. It’s slim, lightweight, and easy to throw into a bag for work or travel. Charging speed has been good so far, and it’s been very reliable.ProsCompact and easy to carry10,000 mAh capacity provides multiple chargesBuilt-in USB-C cable doubles as a strapBraided cable feels sturdyUseful built-in flashlightOverall, this is a very practical portable charger that solves the biggest annoyance with power banks — forgetting a cable. I would definitely recommend it for everyday use, travel, or keeping in a bag just in case.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great value, two gotchas if just wanting plain USB-C power not for charging.
top_reviews.review_body: I bought two of these to run 4 led tower light effects to use where a plug wasnt available, battey life has been great ao far. The only issue is, when you first turn on the battery, it will stay on for a bit with no load, however after it has been on for a while, if the draw goes to almost nothing for even a half second, the battery shuts off. Good for charging so it turns off when done charging, but not a light that may hit a dark pattern, all leds off, the battery shuts off. (I just switched it to a pattern that always has something lit, and it stayed on for hours.) For directly powering something via usb-c, it must need to communicate with the device, so my led lights would not power from them, i had to use the USB A plug for that. This was disappointing as i wanted to power 2 lights from each battery and it only has one USB-A port. Also the LED flashlight, TBH, a joke, only good for ligting up maybe up to 4" away from the light. I do love that they incorporated a USB-C cable as the carrying stap. I didnt even realize it at first. Great value though!

This is the perfect battery pack to have with you for travel. Bought this for my overseas trip and this was a life saver. It's lightweight enough to carry in your pocket. The cable becomes a holder and when needed you just remove it and plug it. It also does fast charging so the phone can be charged very quickly. Overall lightweight, small and has a very good capacity for at least a couple of full phone charges.

We purchased two of these for a vacation to Japan. We were on the move all day, every day. These were a life saver! They charge quickly, have the cord easily accessible, connect perfectly and are adorable. I love the way the paw print lights up and you can see how much the battery is charged. The price is great, too. Any time we take trips, these go along with us. We have used them several times over the past 4 months and they are reliable and fantastic.

top_reviews.review_headline: Reliable Travel Companion — Keeps My Devices Charged All Day!
top_reviews.review_body: I absolutely love this power bank! It’s the perfect mix of compact and powerful the 10,000 mAh capacity easily gave my phone multiple full charges when I was out for the day, which is exactly what I needed for travel and long outings.The slim design makes it incredibly easy to throw in a purse, backpack, or even a pocket without adding bulk, but it still packs enough juice to keep my phone, earbuds, and other devices going when outlets aren’t available.I also appreciate the PowerIQ charging technology it intelligently delivers fast, optimized power to my phone and tablet, so charging feels quick and efficient. The USB-C input/output is super convenient, making recharging the power bank and charging other devices a breeze.Overall, this has become my go-to portable charger for daily use, travel, and work great build quality, dependable performance, and excellent value for the price. Highly recommend!

This Anker PowerCore slim 10000 is a fantastic charger to add to your edc! I own several Anker products and they don't disappoint. 1.It does a good job of charging my phone quickly, 2. sufficient power, can get 2 full charges on it, 3. and can also charge multiple devices at once, before having to cap it off. 4. It’s slim, 5.compact design, 6. and easy to carry at 8.6 oz, and works every time, talk about reliability!7. This PowerCore charger also has a Trickle-Charging Mode: Double-press the button to activate (Green Led) for small devices like Bluetooth headphones and Blue led to indicate charging your phone or larger items such as a laptop. 8. This charger doesn't have a digital gauge to read the battery percentage but it does have colored Indicators: Four LEDs show remaining battery life (25% each x 4) 9. Includes USB-A (output) and USB-C (input/output), with some older models having Micro-USB input.Cons: None!I hope the review helps, from an honest handymans perspective definitely one of the best portable chargers I’ve owned. Do i recommend it? Highly recommended!
Compact but powerful, this power bank delivers. Charges up quickly with USB-C and keeps your devices going. This is my second one, my wife needed one too!

I've bought and used many Anker products, most of them I've only replaced as battery charger wattage requirements have increased. When I needed a power bank for work this was a no-brainer.Only used it a few times, but I think in a pinch you can charge most phones about twice off of one charge of this battery pack.I didn't notice it, or my phone getting warm while it was in use, and my phone is able to turbocharge off of this power bank.It does take a little while to recharge the power bank (as one would expect with a large power bank) even with a 56W charger. It also has a trickle charge feature for some lower wattage power needs such as remotes or other smaller battery devices that won't charge off high wattage chargers, which is nice.My only complaint is that the finish on the back of the power bank scratched almost immediately upon setting it down, which, doesn't effect usability at all. If they would have textured the back of the thing the same as the front it probably wouldn't have happened. That's really the only negative I could come up with so far. Hopefully this battery bank holds up in the long run, but time will tell.

top_reviews.review_headline: 10 Years with Anker — Still My Go-To Power Bank
top_reviews.review_body: Anker has been my go-to brand for power banks for years — our first one was purchased about a decade ago for air travel (back when keeping kids’ devices charged mid-flight was the difference between a peaceful trip and total meltdown — pre seat-outlet days 😅). We’ve stuck with Anker ever since.This is just the latest one we’ve purchased, and it’s exactly what I expect from the brand. It’s compact, reliable, and holds a solid charge, making it great for travel, sports weekends, and everyday backup when you’re on the go. Charges devices quickly and has been consistently dependable.This is one of those items you don’t think about much — until you really need it. And then you’re very glad you have it.

Does what it needs to be, it's slim, fits in my hiking pack well (one small compartment) and works well with my long cord. It's no hiking power bank but it does the job for couple hour hikes and car rides. Don't expect it to be blazing fast charge or a crazy amount of long hour charging storage, get something else for that. This does job well enough.

Nice and slim so it fits easily in a travel bag or hunting and fishing pack.Great power output and super reliable for charging my camera gear, DJI devices, and even my computer. Perfect for being out in the field.

The power bank is reliable, works well, and is great for travel, fitting easily in purses. While some customers find it lightweight, others mention it's slightly heavy to carry in pockets.

top_reviews.review_headline: Major Upgrade from the Previous Version – Smaller, Lighter, Same Power
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve actually owned the first version of this battery pack, and the difference with this newer model is immediately noticeable. The original one I had was significantly bigger and bulkier, so I was honestly surprised (in a good way) that this updated version is much more compact while still delivering the same performance and specifications. It feels like a true upgrade rather than just a refresh.From what I experienced, the older, larger version was eventually recalled, so it’s reassuring to see that this newer model not only addresses those concerns but improves the overall design.Performance wise, it does exactly what you want from a high quality battery pack. It charges devices quickly and consistently, holds a solid charge over time, and is reliable when you need it most. I’ve used it for my phone and other devices, and it handles multiple charges without any issues.A few things I really like:• Much more portable and easier to carry around compared to the previous version• Solid build quality that feels durable and well made• Fast and efficient charging• Great capacity without the added bulk• Perfect for travel, work, or just keeping in your bag for everyday useOverall, this is a big improvement over the original. If you were hesitant because of how bulky older versions were, this one fixes that completely while still delivering the same power. Definitely worth it.Added photo next to wallet for scale

I’ve been using this ANKER portable charger consistently and it’s solid across the board. Durability is on point, it feels sturdy and not cheap at all, I throw it in my bag daily and it holds up. The built-in cord is short but that’s actually perfect for portability, it’s made to charge while you’re holding your phone or keeping it in your pocket, not stretch across the room. Connectivity is smooth, once you plug it in it starts charging instantly with no disconnect issues. Compatibility is easy, works perfectly with my USB-C devices without any extra setup. The charger quality feels premium, no overheating or slow charging. As for power capacity, it gives me a full charge with some power left over, which is clutch for long days out. Overall it’s compact, reliable, and gets the job done.

Seems like a solid, sturdy product! I ordered the white, the labeling on the back of the unit in grey/silver is a little light, but readable. It came in packaging which spoke "quality." Carefully and precisely wrapped. The unit was 24% charged when I opened it. It fits in the palm of my hand, but it would be a little heavy for a pocket. Purchased for travel in carry-on. I need this to charge an Apple iPhone 14 Plus. The built-in adapter did not fit the cords for my phone. The directions state to use the same brand cords (Anker). I ended up ordering the Anker USB Type C Lightning Cable Upcycled Braided cord and the Anker Nano USBC Charger Block, 30w. With these two, I was able to charge the Anker Portable Charger to 100% capacity in about an hour. Then I plugged in my iPhone and charged it to 100% . The unit is new, so I haven't tried out the battery life yet. First impressions are a quality, well-made unit.

top_reviews.review_headline: The Only Power Bank Brand We Trust—Fast, Reliable, and Built to Last
top_reviews.review_body: We currently own three different Anker portable chargers, and they have become absolute essentials for our family. The most impressive thing about them is the longevity; even after a couple of years of heavy use, they still hold a significant charge.The charging speed is excellent—it’s noticeably faster than the generic banks we've tried in the past, easily getting our phones back to 50% in about 30 minutes. Whether we are traveling or just away from an outlet for the day, these chargers are reliable, compact, and worth every penny. If you want a 'set it and forget it' piece of tech, this is it.

top_reviews.review_headline: Not perfect, but closer to the target performance than most other alternatives at a great price.
top_reviews.review_body: Okay, let us cover the good part first: It has great PD compatibility. It can do 5V@3A, 9V@3A, 10V@ 2A, 12V@2.5A, 15V@2A, 20V@1.5A. So, that is more than many other power pack out there. (however there are some limitations that I will get to in the later section). The output is max of 30W (I confirmed it). It says it is 10Ah but that is rated at 3.7V which means it is only 37Wh. I have drained it from 100% to 0% and managed to draw 31Wh out of the pack that is rated for 37Wh. That is 84% which is better than just about all the power packs out there that I have experienced. Most other power packs out there can barely do 50% of the rated capacity or 65% on the better ones. The display is nice to know what % of capacity remaining. A sustained draw of 2A @5V will make the pack fairly warm to touch. The USB-C cable that comes with the pack is nice to have. The size is also fairly compact which is easier to carry.Now, let us talk about the down sides: I cycled my first power pack and tried to measure the power rating. The pack PD outputs died after draining it on a constant load of 30W (1.5A @20V) for about 10 minutes. After that, it can only deliver 5V/2A. Then the cabled terminal shows "low voltage". I am guessing the USB PD IC died inside the power pack. So, Amazon sent me a replacement unit. So far that unit has worked without dying yet. But I would keep an eye on the unit if high power dissipation is needed. I was loading the replacement unit at 1.5A@15V, once it drained to about 25% in capacity, it no longer can sustain that output. Those voltage/amp ratings are really only correct when battery is more than 50% in capacity (that is expected for most lithium ion batteries where peak current output will fall as capacity reduces). The 5V/2A output has no problem sustaining it until 0%. Charging is also not as high as it is indicated. On the back of the unit, it says 5V/3A charging rate, the unit can only sustain 5V/2A charging rate. So, a full charge will take about 3 hours.Overall, it is a decent power pack. I am a fan of Anker products. Although it isn't perfect or delivering everything it is claiming, it is way closer to its claims than just about any other power packs I have used. If the application only needs 5V/2A(very popular mode), then it can last 3 hours easily.General complain, I really don't like how power packs are rated or advertised by xxxxmAh. Why don't we just use Ah? 1000mA is 1A. Isn't that simpler? Also, those ratings are misleading. The Wh is much more accurate. Of course, most packs can't deliver those energy level at all the voltages. So, it is another misleading information. At least Anker has delivered the real world rating that are closer to their claim than others.And for the price, I think it is a great value. So I would recommend getting it over other brands. If a 30Wh pack (5V/2A for 3 hours) is good enough for the application, then I think this is the one to get.

top_reviews.review_body: This power bank is awesome. It takes about 8 hours to fully charge from empty. I just plug it in while im resting. However , when it's charged it can power my laptop, phone and earbuds , at the same time. I usually charge it to 100% once a week. It will charge my phone multiple times (Z Fold 5) It is a little "chunky". But it is a 60K MAH. :)

top_reviews.review_headline: Packs a Mighty Punch for Its Size
top_reviews.review_body: I recently purchased this charger to provide extra power during the charging process for my iPad and iPhone (and possibly my MacBook if the need should ever arise) while traveling. My previous one ALWAYS burned hot as it overexerted itself in recharging larger devices. Straight out of the box, the power bank was at a 70% charge. While it took some time to reach a 100% charge (with a 40-watt plug and fast-speed cord), I assumed the first full charge time rate was normal (especially considering its size), and it remained cool as it was charging. It is by no means lightweight enough for use in a purse all day (advertised as being 15.5 oz, but actually was 1 lb 0.4 oz), but it feels plenty solid for a power bank of its size, and sits just fine in my vehicle’s cup holder.For those who like to investigate items before purchasing by reading one-star reviews, I want to share some thoughts in response to those complaining that this portable charger cannot be carried on a plane. While that is true, nowhere does the seller indicate that it should be. Rather, if the purchasers had taken the time to read all of the product description, it clearly states in capital letters, “(IMPORTANT: 60000MAH PORTABLE CHARGER ARE NOT ALLOWED ON AIRPLANE)” From my perspective, it seems both counterintutive and unfair to rank a product at one star when the buyer purchases an item that clearly advertises it is not FDA approved and then after purchasing the item anyway then complains it is not approved for airplanes.Another thing to consider. While I can understand how one cannot anticipate the weight by reading a number description, it does not seem appropriate to mark down stars for weight simply because, after receiving it, you determine it will not meet your personal needs. I anticipated that the weight of the power charger would correspond to its capacity and size. I often carry around a 16-inch laptop, so this charger felt light in comparison. I have no problems carrying it around for what I plan to use it for.The convenient removable charging cord provides a way to charge devices with the most common port connectors (USB-C, iOS, and Micro USB). You can also use your own charging cord plugged directly into the power charger’s USB-A or USB-C ports. The directions indicate that one of the USB-A ports can be used in low-current devices (Ex, AirPods, headphones, and watches). Overall, the charger works sufficiently for its intended purpose. I charged my iPhone 13 from 38% to 96% in one hour and sixteen minutes (somewhat average rate for using a 22.5-watt portable charger with this phone model, not using a fast charge cord). The portable charger dropped from 99% to 89% in that one charging cycle. I haven’t tried it with my iPad yet, but I believe it should work exceptionally well with that as well.

I have no complaints. The picture of the display screen shows a bit excess light, however I really like the display screen. Screen is 10/10. Functionality is a 10/10. Has a single button to light the display screen, charging is automatic. It did come with the 1to3 cable (not pictured). It is wider and heavier than typical battery pack. I have not noticed any excess heat. Seems to charge at an adequate speed, and charges my 2 iPhones for gaming, and my LED screen Backpack. (I have a Nyan Gear pack that requires a USB-A (normal usb) to function, and the pack stays on while using) I dont use the USB-C output.
All around good power bank. Takes a bit to charge up being as big as it is, but lasts a long time and has been efficient overall.

top_reviews.review_headline: Massive Capacity and Reliable Power – Great for Travel and Multiple Devices
top_reviews.review_body: I picked up this 60000mAh portable charger because I needed something that could last a long time without constantly recharging, and it has definitely delivered. The battery life is excellent and easily powers phones, tablets, and other devices multiple times before needing a recharge.Performance has been very consistent. It charges devices reliably and holds its charge well when not in use, which makes it especially useful for travel, long days out, or keeping in an emergency kit. Even with heavier use, it still has plenty of reserve capacity.The only small downside is the included multi-end charging cable. While it’s convenient to have, I do wish it was either integrated into the power bank or had a dedicated storage slot so it wouldn’t get misplaced. That would make the overall design even more practical.Overall, it’s a solid, high-capacity power bank that works exactly as expected.

The color is super bright and pretty, the shipping is fast, directions are super simple, and it says it works with multiple devices. It feels really heavy and durable, which makes me think it will last a while. I'm crazy excited to use this.
Very nice and not as bulky or heavy as I anticipated. I really like that it came with the cables to charge any phone.

top_reviews.review_headline: Charges up fast and charges phones fast.
top_reviews.review_body: This charger works as expected. Would have given this 5 starts but the only draw back is the cords have no connection and can easily be misplaced. The unit charges quickly and holds a charge for a long period of time. It charges my phone in quick order. With all said, It is a heavy charger and can be a bit cumbersome. However, seems to be very well put together. I would recommend buy this charger. I'll update later on it's longevity.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great features, high capacity and rate of charge
top_reviews.review_body: Bought primarily for multi week backpacking trip in the mountains. The main feature that drew me to this product is the rate at which this battery bank can be recharged. It is capable of being charged at 100W and will fill in 1 hour and 40 minutes, which is great. I wanted this specifically because I knew my time available to actually recharge this might be short and couldn't wait overnight so I bought the Anker 100W charger as well. The do indeed work together perfectly and I can confirm I have charged fully several times now and its tome to recharge is as it should be. I also liked that I can see exactly what is going on with the charger whether I am recharging the battery or charging other devices or both, the display tells me exactly what power is being delivered to each output. I found this useful while managing power on trail to recharge phone, head lamp, and watch. The built in cables work well and I have no complains about them, though I think I would have chosen the product without the built in cables, this one happened to be on sale when I bought it, I can go either way on the built in cables, they aren't problematic, if you are counting grams you might make a different choice. It charged my iPhone 16e at about 18W but seemed to vary the speed it was charging it, I think thats just part of the battery management and power delivery protocols in place for the device, in any case it charged rapidly.For my purposes weight was a major issue and this is a heavy choice, further made more difficult by requiring a 100w charger, which is heavy, to quickly charge this device. I calculate .84 oz/aH for this device and compare that to what I think is the lightest weight device at .51 oz/aH for the 20k version from a popular other manufacturer for ultralight backpacking gear. That device has none of the features this Anker product has and is not capable of fast charging, taking about 8 hours to recharge.All in all after a couple weeks in the mountains with this, I can say I am glad I had it. The newer iPhones are remarkable on battery usage when you are careful to control airplane mode, gps, etc. I even had enough power to let myself watch some movies occasionally without worry about running out. When I got to a place I could recharge I was able to relatively quickly charge up without leaving the gear laying there all day. I have used many Anker products in the past and this has been no different, it appears to obey standards (ie it seem to negotiate successfully with the iPhone on rate of recharge) and seems durable and works as advertised. There are new products already from Anker so take a look at those if you are shopping for something like this, I think the tech is changing rapidly. If this product meets your needs I think you are safe in purchasing it, I have been very happy. I just wish it could magically weigh much less but the tech just isn't there yet.Works great, features are excellent, reliable, fast, would buy again.

top_reviews.review_headline: Excellent all-around power bank with fast charging and a great display
top_reviews.review_body: I picked up this Anker power bank for use with multiple devices and it has performed very well both at home and while traveling. Charging speeds are excellent across the board. It charges my MacBook Air M4 at up to 67W, my Steam Deck OLED at 45W, and my iPhone 15 Pro at around 20W, which is exactly what I was hoping for.In terms of size, it’s not pocketable, but it’s also not unwieldy. It’s easy to throw into a bag and is perfect for using around the house when you’re not near an outlet, like in the backyard, as well as for travel days when access to power is limited.One of my favorite features is the display. It clearly shows battery status and real-time charging data, including charging speeds, which is far more informative than basic LED indicators and genuinely useful when managing multiple devices.I’ve used several Anker products over the years, including other power banks and wall chargers, and this continues that trend of solid build quality and reliable performance. Overall, this has become my go-to portable charger for higher-power devices, and I would confidently recommend it to anyone looking for a capable, high-capacity power bank.

I love this charger; I bring it with me every time I leave the area. The size of this power bank is pretty big; it would barely fit in a pocket, so I usually carry a bag. I love the two built-in cords. I go out with my friends, so I can charge my phone and theirs without having to worry about an outlet since we usually go somewhere where it's not available. USB-C is really great since a lot of devices are using this, so I can charge all my devices with this one power bank. The screen is more like fun to see, in my opinion. Other than battery health and percent remaining, the rest is just fun data to see. I sometimes test how fast some cables are with it. Though I haven't tried charging it with the two cables, I would assume they could do that too (edit, it does not, it only accepts ones). The battery lasts days without losing charge (when not using, saying it doesn't lose charge when not in use). It charges things really fast too, almost as fast as the device lets it. Overall, it's fast, big, and all I need to make sure I feel assured that my phone or laptop doesn't die.

top_reviews.review_headline: Best Anker Power Bank I’ve Ever Owned
top_reviews.review_body: The best power bank I ever owned. It was so good that someone got to friendly with my original one therefore, I had to purchase another. The charges are super fast and it ever over heats. Love Anker products. Fits right into my bag, very compact and offers two USB cords. I chose the black one because it looks very sleek. It even has a happy face 🙂 Great product

I was a little hesitant about buying this because the photos make it look larger than it is. I can say it is very solid and feels like a high quality product. The built-in handle/cable is very durable and such a brilliant idea. And the retractable USB-C cable is a thin, but quality cable as well. So convenient to have these already attached and it becomes apparent that the company has spent a lot of time using and designing this product. The display is incredible and very informative as well. I can say this product is worth its price. Well done Anker! Highly Recommended.

I bought this on my trip to japan and the use of it during the month till now is amazing. the price isn't too bad for the quality you get as the charging ports are usb-c which is perfect and compatible for most devices. the size is pretty good as it fits in any bag nicely but the weight of it is definitely on the heavier side. the battery life for charging phones and such is good but laptops only one charge which is fine as I don't use it like that

top_reviews.review_headline: Great everyday power bank with built-in cables (huge plus)
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve used a few portable chargers over the years, and this one stands out for convenience.The built-in cables are honestly the best part. No extra cords to carry, no digging through a bag when your phone is dying. Everything is already attached and ready to use, which makes it great for travel or long days out.It also handles multiple devices well. I’ve been able to charge my phone and another device at the same time without any issues, and the charging speed stays consistent.The digital display is a nice upgrade from the usual indicator lights. Being able to see the exact battery percentage is much more useful.Battery life has been solid so far. It easily gets me through multiple charges, and having two in the pack means I always have one ready as backup.It’s also slim and easy to carry, which makes a difference if you’re using it daily.The only minor downside is that the built-in cables could be a bit longer depending on how you’re using it, but that’s a small trade-off for the convenience.Overall, very practical and easy to use. Definitely a good option if you want something simple and reliable.

top_reviews.review_headline: Saves space, lightweight, and very efficient!!
top_reviews.review_body: I absolutely love this item. Discharging bank is superior compared to the other charging banks I’ve bought in the past. It has cords already built into the back and a charging cable attached to it as well. The charging bank has additional ports that you can use with your own cords if you would like. I highly recommend this item and we’ll be buying it as a gift for others in the future.

top_reviews.review_headline: Very pleased with these battery packs so far
top_reviews.review_body: Edit - I've been using these for a few months now I think, not exactly sure how long. I still really like them. As a matter of fact, I bought two more. I accidentally snapped the head off of one of the charge cords that are built in. Entirely my fault and took considerable force, very stupid; since it was my mistake I decided to simply buy another pair rather than try to take advantage of the 3 year warranty. The one with the broken cord will live in the car for emergencies. I think these are great little battery packs and use them almost every day.------Original review -I really like these battery packs! It's a great price for a two pack, the integrated cords are very convenient, and they feel very solidly constructed. I always keep two battery packs, but it never occurred to me to get two different colors to tell them apart. Makes it easier to grab whichever is currently fully charged when I leave the house.I was not aware that they have a three-year warranty when I purchased them but I'm very pleased; my last battery packs were a well-established, somewhat expensive brand but one got swollen after about a year and a half, so a long warranty means a lot. They're also pretty compact for 12,800 mAh; same size as my previous 10,000mAh packs (from the "good" brand I owned previously).

top_reviews.review_headline: Great value, super convenient for travel and everyday use
top_reviews.review_body: Great value and super convenient. The built-in cables mean no extra cords to carry, and the battery lasts for multiple charges. Slim, lightweight, and perfect for travel. Charging speed is solid too. Definitely recommend! 👍

top_reviews.review_headline: Massive Battery Life That Keeps You Powered for Days!
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve tried quite a few portable chargers over the years, but this 50000mAh power bank really stands out. If you’re someone who’s always on the go or hates constantly worrying about battery life, this is a game changer.First off, the capacity is incredible—you can charge your phone multiple times (seriously, multiple days’ worth) before needing to recharge the power bank itself. It’s perfect for travel, long commutes, camping trips, or even just keeping at home as a backup during power outages.The build quality feels solid and durable, not cheap or flimsy. Even though it’s a high-capacity charger, it’s still reasonably portable for what it offers. The multiple charging ports are super convenient—you can charge more than one device at the same time without any issues.Charging speed is impressive too. It powers up devices quickly and efficiently, which is a huge plus when you’re in a hurry. I also appreciate the LED display—it clearly shows the remaining battery percentage, so you’re never guessing.Overall, this is a reliable, high-capacity power bank that delivers exactly what it promises. If you need something powerful and dependable, this is absolutely worth it. Highly recommend!

top_reviews.review_headline: Powerful, Convenient, and Perfect for Travel
top_reviews.review_body: This 50,000mAh portable charger is a lifesaver, especially for travel and long days away from an outlet. The battery capacity is huge I can charge my phone multiple times and still have power left. The 22.5W fast charging works great and noticeably speeds up charging compared to standard power banks.I really love the built-in cables. Not having to carry extra cords is super convenient, and the cables feel sturdy and well made. The digital display showing charging wattage is a nice touch and makes it easy to track performance. Despite its large capacity, it’s surprisingly compact and easy to carry in a bag or backpack.It works perfectly with my iPhone and also charges other devices like Android phones and tablets without any issues. The carbon black design looks sleek and premium.

top_reviews.review_headline: Worry free charging for long days
top_reviews.review_body: Great customer service for this product when I needed help choosing the best power bank for my upcoming trip. No worries about running out of juice now!
Super fast charging! My whole family loves it! I bought 5 of them!!! Perfect for road trips, vacations, planes! Very powerful compact portable charger!
So far working like a charm. Charges for 8 hours.

On a Friday afternoon during high winds, we lost power at my house. In the 23 years in this house, only once did we lose power for one hour previously. That day, we were without power for a total of 3.5 hours. I have an autistic daughter who was struggling through it because she likes her cartoons and movies. I found an old very small power bank that I could keep her iPad alive with so she could listen to music and I used my new iPad to charge my phone. I found ways to get by but decided I better start working on some ideas of how to get by without power and keep her occupied. BIG power banks. This was the one I chose to give the first test.I received this on a Saturday afternoon and immediately started charging it. It had a charge but still took a few hours to top off. I used it to charge my iPhone 13, both apple watches, air pods and even did a one time charge on two iPads. I will start off by saying that whenever I was in my car, which isn't much, or in bed sleeping, I plug my phone in to charge. With that said, starting that Saturday night, this portable charger was all I was using to charge my devices. Every night before bed I would charge my phone and watch to 100%. I would charge my air pods when they needed it and I use those all the time. When appropriate, I used the built in USB-C and Apple Lightning cords but there is a USB-C and a USB-A port so you can plug your own cords in, such as for charging the watch. To give an idea of the size, it comfortably fits in my hand. It has a display to tell you what % charge is left in the bank.So I started using this exclusively from that Saturday, (other than phone when in car or sleeping) and it finally ran out of power the following Friday morning. I actually used it for charging devices for 7 days.Now I am letting is sit with a full charge for a few weeks and then will use it exclusively for charging again to see if the performance is the same. If it is, I will most likely buy 1 maybe 2 more. I don't have any reference to compare it to, but it impressed me all the same.
Comes in handy it's a good little power pack

I like this charger because it has the cords built in. I do feel like it’s a bit heavy but not too bad to travel with. I’m hoping the battery life is long as I’ve only had it for a month or so but so far so good.

top_reviews.review_headline: Slim, Durable, and Powerful Enough for My Laptop, Steam Deck, and iPad
top_reviews.review_body: I really like this Power Bank. For the amount of power it holds, it’s surprisingly slim and lightweight, which makes it perfect for travel or everyday carry. It slips into my bag easily without adding bulk, and the built-in cable is a huge bonus — no extra cords needed.Durability-wise, it feels solid and well-made. The finish and color give it a clean, modern look, and it holds up well even when I toss it around in my work or travel bag. It still looks great after regular use.The battery life is excellent. I can charge multiple devices on a single cycle, and the 45W output is strong enough to handle more than just a phone. I’ve used it to charge my laptop, Steam Deck, and iPad, and it handles them surprisingly well. My Steam Deck especially benefits from the fast charging, and my iPad charges quickly and reliably.For something this slim and portable, the performance really stands out. Between the durability, battery life, and the ability to power all my main devices, this power bank is absolutely worth it.

Really happy with this UGREEN 45W power bank. It’s small, easy to carry, and charges super fast. I use it for my phone and other small devices, and it even tops up my laptop when needed. Feels well made and reliable. Great for travel or everyday use. Weight is around 400g, has built in type c and an extra type c to type c cable. It doesn’t have CCC(required for domestic China airplanes) so be careful.This is my 2nd power bank as the previous one got stolen so im happy to buy it again

top_reviews.review_headline: Awesome power bank, wroth purchase again.
top_reviews.review_body: Fast charging, portable and overall great item. I took this on a 2 week vacation to Japan, along with another charger. Never ended up needing the extra one. This power bank is durable, and kept all my electronics charged (phone, camera, translation device, portable wifi, etc.)The attached USB-C plug is a game changer as I never needed to worry about making sure U had a cable. The digital readout keys you see how much you charge you have left. This will be my go to charger for the foreseeable future. I also bought the UGREEN bluetooth transmitter and like that item as well. Seems like a reliable, well made brand.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Power Bank for the Steam Deck!
top_reviews.review_body: I've used this power bank over the last few weeks and really like it. I like that it comes with a built-in cable, so it makes charging devices convenient and easy.I charged my phone and steam deck with this power bank and it charges quickly! This power bank is a bit bulky and heavy, but I don't mind since it comes with a large battery capacity and 45W.It's more affordable compared to the other power banks I've seen with this battery capacity. I got this for only $30, so for this price it's really great. The material feels high-quality.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great Power Bank with Minor Trade-Off
top_reviews.review_body: This 20000mAh 45W power bank performs very well and delivers fast, reliable charging. The built-in cable is super convenient and makes it easy to carry without extra cords. It charges devices quickly and holds plenty of power for multiple uses. The only downside is that it's a little bulky, so it's not the most pocket-friendly option. Overall, it's a solid, high-quality portable charger and definitely worth it if you don't mind the extra size.

top_reviews.review_headline: Long battery life and fast charging
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve been using this power bank for about a week and I’m impressed with the battery capacity. It charges my phone multiple times without needing to recharge it. The fast charging works well and the build quality feels solid. The only downside is that it’s a little heavy, but that’s normal for a high-capacity power bank. Overall, it’s a good product for travel and daily use. I would recommend it.

This is an absolute unit of a power bank so far.The weight can be a bit much for some cases, and I see that, but for a battery pack that is belt-clipped for VR gaming, this has been an absolute dream purchase. You can feel how well designed it is when you first pick it up. No rattling, no flimsy feel, and the built in wire has some serious durable lining to it.Lives true to it's charging abilities so far and I will make sure to update the post after a couple hundred hours of use.Picture provided to assist with size comparison. Standard size logitech mouse.

top_reviews.review_body: I have a few ANKER products and I LOVE this power bank. It's built well and charges my phone fast!! The digital display is bright and shows how much power is left and that's super handy. The USB-C cord snaps in place when not needed and it's held up great with daily use in the year I've had it. It's heavy but that's because it's 20,000 mAh. It gets tossed in my work bag every day and has worked flawlessly. I definitely received this product!

Works great with Steam Deck, charges the device super fast. The cable being in-laid with the shell is very handy. The battery display is easy to read and seems accurate. The spare USB ports at the top (female USB-C and female USB-A) both are also ultra fast for charging and discharging. I like that this unit is slimmer but wider as it fits in my storage case much better than one of the square units.

ProsLightweight charger of good size that charges fast and recharges pretty fastConsSeems like when it charges it discharges pretty fast, haven't figured because this charges at 65 W if this doesn't put out enough power or it's my cable but doesn't charge my S26 Ultra at Super Fast Charging 3.0 as I intended it to. Could be my cableOversl really good lightweight charger that charges fast and gives your phone or Laptop a good boost

top_reviews.review_headline: Loving this portable charger for the past nine months! I don't leave home without it.
top_reviews.review_body: I have been a proud owner of my Anker portable charger for nine months. It mainly lives in my EDC bag, but has also gotten used in the home when my youngest child is using her phone but not near an electrical outlet and her battery is low. It came in handy for my teen who needed to have a backup charging source with her for computer-based standardized testing. I feel good knowing that if there's a power outage, I have a reliable charger for my Android phone, MacBook, and Bose headphones (when in trickle charge mode for low current devices). And, when we go out of town...this charger ALWAYS tags along.I love the built-in charging cable—that's a gold feature in itself. I have a few other chargers, but this is my primary for that reason. If something were to happen to it, I would purchase it again. It recharges quickly with my 45W charging block using either the built-in cable or a separate USB-C cable. I am considering buying both of my children one for Christmas this year to replace their Anker chargers (that don't have a built-in cable nor provide as many charges).What's not to love? It's compatible with my devices, provides me with multiple charges, it's reliable, and I love the Anker quality.Update (2/12/26): I bought the violet Anker for one of my daughters for Christmas (see photo). I also purchased a case for mine recently (purchased on Amazon and reviewed separately; it offers nice protection, but it's hard to catch in stock).https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0DGFRD1JB?ref=ppx_yo2ov_dt_b_fed_asin_title

Outstanding product! I like the minimalist U.I. - just one button to push to turn the screen on, & a double click to put it in/out of the trickle charge mode (which you'd never need to do if you just use the USB-C port/cord to charge lower power devices like headphones). It charges my phones as fast as any high quality wall charger, & can charge my phone a solid 3 times before it's needing a recharge itself! I like that there's a built in cord, & especially LOVE that the cord can be tucked securely into the side of the power bank! So many other power banks use their built in cords as a "lanyard" which I really just don't think is a wise idea. Not only is it more stress carrying it around by that cable, but I also don't want a idiotic lanyard hanging off the side & getting snagged on things when taking it in/out of cases, drawers, bags, etc... When I came across this model, it's neatly tucked away cable is what sold me! Couldn't ask for a more perfect power bank!This might not be for you if you want slim & light for pocket carrying as it's a bit heavy. But if you aren't planning to carry this in your pants pocket, the weight it not an issue at all.

Great charger/power bank. Used for several brands of phones, portable games, flashlights, etc. Super fast charging my Samsung Galaxy s23 and s24 ultra without issue. Huge capacity. I like having the cord attached. Nothing to have to remember when going out. Just grab n go. It's a tad heavy and bigger than some other 20,000mah power banks so only 4 stars from me. Highly recommend if you don't care about size.

Worked great during a seven hour layover between long flights. I was able to charge all of my devices (iPad, phone, headphones, kindle) quickly and the power bank didn't even need a recharge before my return flight. The built in cable made charging easy.

Premium Design and Durability: Featuring a sleek matte finish, a sturdy metal frame, and aerogel thermal insulation for cool operation, this power bank combines ergonomic and user-friendly experience with long-lasting durability.
What You Get: Anker MagGo Power Bank (10K, Slim), 23.62" / 60 cm USB-C to USB-C cable, welcome guide, 24-month warranty, and our friendly customer service.

top_reviews.review_headline: Slim, strong magnet, easy to carry
top_reviews.review_body: I bought this mainly because I wanted a thin magnetic power bank that I could just snap onto the back of my phone. The magnet is strong and lines up easily, so I don’t have to fiddle with positioning every time.The thing I like most is the size and thickness. For a 10,000mAh battery it’s relatively slim, so it doesn’t feel like a huge brick hanging off the back of the phone. It’s easy to carry in a bag or pocket, and it’s convenient when I’m out for the day. A 10,000mAh pack like this can usually recharge a phone around one to almost two times for my iphone 17.Charging speed is solid. The Qi2 wireless charging goes up to 15W and there’s also a USB-C port for faster wired charging if you need it. But it does get warm sometimes.Wireless charging does drain the power bank faster than using a cable. For me the convenience of snapping it on makes up for that. I mostly use it on long days out or when traveling, and it’s been very reliable so far.

This Anker MagGo Power Bank is fantastic! It’s ultra-slim, portable, and the magnetic attachment makes charging my devices so convenient.The only minor downside is that it does get a little warm during use, but nothing concerning. Overall, it’s reliable, powerful, and perfect for on-the-go charging.

I’ve been using this power bank for a bit now and it’s been really convenient. The size is one of the best parts, it’s super slim and easy to carry around without feeling bulky in a pocket or bag.The magnetic connection works well and snaps right into place. It stays secure while charging, which I was a little worried about at first. Charging speed is solid and it gives my phone a good boost when I need it during the day.Battery life has been reliable so far and it’s nice not having to carry cables all the time.Only small thing is it can get a little warm while charging, but nothing unusual. Overall, really happy with it.

Works great. A quick charge, so probably not great to use all the time as it's probably not great for phone battery life, but very convenient for airport/travel use. No cords to keep track of, and the magnet strongly attaches the battery to your phone.

This power bank charges with USB-C although it is called slim, I have seen other 10,000 mA batteries that are a smaller in form factor. It connects easily to my iPhone, using the magnet connector. It also charges my phone very quickly. I have noticed. The downside, however is that both the battery on the phone get hard very quickly. My solution to this has been to charge the phone to about 60% and then remove the power bank and while the battery still gets warm, it’s not overly so. For the price, it’s a great value.

Absolutely fantastic power bank. The Anker MagGo is incredibly slim and lightweight, yet still packs a full 10,000mAh capacity. It feels premium in hand and attaches securely with strong magnets — no slipping or misalignment.The Qi2 15W charging is impressively fast for a magnetic battery pack. My phone charges quickly and consistently without overheating. It’s perfect for travel, long days out, or just having reliable backup power.What really stands out is the build quality. The finish feels durable and high-end, exactly what you’d expect from Anker. It doesn’t add unnecessary bulk to the phone, and it fits comfortably in a pocket or bag.Overall, this is one of the best magnetic power banks I’ve used. Powerful, sleek, and reliable. Highly recommended.

name: Power Bank, 15000mAh Portable Charger with Built in Cable, 22.5W Fast Charging, 6 Outputs, Slim Travel Essential Battery Pack with LED Display, Compatible with iPhone 17/16/15/14/13, iPad Black
description:
Portable Charger with Built-in Cables for Multiple Devices: This portable charger includes integrated USB A, Type C, IOS, and Micro cables, making it compatible with iPhone 17/16/15/14/13/12, Samsung Galaxy, Google Pixel, iPads, tablets, headphones, smartwatches, and more. Perfect for work, travel, or camping, it eliminates the need for multiple cables and adapters, providing reliable power for a seamless travel experience.
22.5W Fast Charging for Maximum Efficiency: This power bank delivers 22.5W output with advanced fast-charging technology, reducing charging time significantly. Its intelligent voltage regulation adjusts to the optimal output for each device, ensuring fast and safe charging. For fast-charging devices, just a few minutes can provide a noticeable power boost. This feature enhances user experience and helps minimize battery wear from extended charging.
Charge Multiple Devices Simultaneously with Bi-Directional Type-C Port: This power bank features 6 output ports and 3 input ports, allowing you to charge up to 6 devices at once, greatly improving charging efficiency. With its upgraded Type-C port, it supports both fast output and input charging, making it a versatile two-way port compatible with all Type-C devices. Enjoy faster, more convenient charging without the long waits, whether for your gadgets or the battery pack itself.
Compact and Portable for Easy Travel: Designed to be compact and lightweight, this portable power bank measures just 4.56 x 2.67 x 0.86 inches and weighs only 7.09 ounces, roughly the size of a small smartphone. It easily fits into your pocket, handbag, or backpack, making it effortless to carry around. Additionally, it comes with a portable lanyard, allowing you to hang it on your bag or purse, freeing up your hands and ensuring your devices are always powered up while on the go.
Smart LED Display for Power Management: The built-in high-definition LED display provides a real-time percentage of remaining battery life and current charging status, offering a clear and straightforward view. The LED screen lets you quickly check the power bank’s battery level, so you never have to guess when it needs recharging. This helps you avoid running out of power and alleviates battery anxiety.
Safe for Air Travel: This portable charger meets safety certifications and complies with airline standards, making it safe for flights. Built with fire-resistant materials and smart protection against over-current, over-voltage, overheating, and overcharging, it's ideal for business trips, vacations, or commutes. Reliable and safe, it ensures worry-free power throughout your journey.

top_reviews.review_headline: 5-Star Review: HELLEE 15000mAh Portable Charger – The “I Forgot My Cables” Savior
top_reviews.review_body: Look, I’m the guy who once boarded a 5-hour flight with 3% battery, no charger cable, and the panic of a man realizing he left his dignity at home.Enter the HELLEE 15000mAh power bank with built-in cables. This thing is basically a portable charger that went to therapy and decided to stop making my life harder.First off, those built-in cables? Chef’s kiss. Four of them pop out like obedient little tentacles: USB-C, Lightning (for us Apple hostages), Micro-USB (for that one ancient device still kicking), and USB-A. No more rummaging through the bag abyss yelling “WHERE IS THE DAMN CABLE?!” at 2 a.m. in an airport. Just yank ‘em out, plug in, and boom—your phone, tablet, earbuds, and probably your neighbor’s Fitbit are all juicing up simultaneously because this bad boy has 6 outputs total.The 22.5W fast charging is no joke either. My iPhone went from “please help I’m dying” to “okay I’m back, let’s doomscroll” in like 20 minutes. top_reviews.review_body: I love love loveeee this portable charger. If you're looking for a portable charger that not only charges your phone, it has a USB port so you can charge you watch and also there is a port for typc c chargers as well that could charge. The power for this charger works tremendously for me! The battery doesn't change too fast. I have an iPhone 14 Pro Max and when this charger is at a full battery, it is expected that it will charge your phone to 100% and still have about 54% of juice left over. It is quite heavy since its a literal block but its the size of my pro max, maybe a lil smaller. I think its compact and able to fit most purses. The heat does intensify but nothing that will rock your socks off.I am on my second one and it will come very faster. I remember i needed a charger asap while on vacation and I was able to purchase another and it came fast. My mom just purchased her fourth so she can give them out as gifts.Safe to say we love this charger and you will too!

For the price point, this charger is great. I got it to replace my Anker USB power bank which did not come with cords. I would have a usbc and an iPhone charger plugged into it at all times and when I wasn't using it I would have to wrap the cords around the charger and stuff it in my bag. I like that these cords tuck away and they are very secure when tucked and won't fall out. It also has its own charger USB attached which is nice. I used to have to flip my usbc around to plug the USB end in to charge my anker. The short length of the cords is a thing to get used to. I can't put the charger in my bag and have the cord long enough to use it while the charger is out of sight. This also makes it so that when it is charging and you are using your phone, in most cases you have to hold the charger in your hand in edition to your phone. Not a huge setback as I just place my phone over top of the charger and hold them as basically one unit. Some people complained about the charging speed of both the charger itself and your phone. I've found that it charges itself faster than my anker and charges my phone just as fast as the anker. I just plug the charger in and leave it overnight and by the morning it has full charge. I never timed how long it took to charge. The digital readout of charge left is nice. The anker had 4 dots and you never knew how close the last dot was to dead. I've had it about 2 weeks now so I can't speak on the longevity of it, but for 20 bucks it does it's job better than the price point I feel. I bought a second one for my sister.
Great product I got my whole family one for Christmas. They take a while to charge, but they will charge my phone about three times.

I love this portable charger! It's cute, works as intended, and has all the chargers you'd need. I take it with me on trips, when I'm out in the city all day, or to concerts. I can charge my phone multiple times before the battery life runs out. The battery life hasn't decreased even after months of using it. My coworker used mine and loved it so much she bought one for herself to take on vacation.

top_reviews.review_headline: Good quality charger for the price
top_reviews.review_body: This portable charger is perfect for on the go and usually can charge my phone twice before needing to be charged itself. My one issue is that the attached chargers stopped working.

Please forgive me I did a second review to correct this because I didn't realize that the top part with the handle is the actual charger I think anyway . I do love it no matter what it takes a long time to charge it seems but you know when you first get a battery that that's usually The wave so far so good but I was wrong I'm sorry I think that a little charger that's folded on the box is the actual handle part is you see coming out .... Get one it's great it's got so many chargers you could I mean it's great because everybody's always saying you have a charger and nobody really has this is forever I use it in the car too

top_reviews.review_headline: My first portable charger - would buy again.
top_reviews.review_body: I bought this for weekends we spend at our kid’s university. He’s in the band and we go to all the home games. We spend all day on campus at pep rallies and campus activities. Photos and videos deplete my battery before the day is over and I can plug in. I bought this to carry in my purse so I can make it to the end of the day as I take lots of photos and videos. Charged my iPhone fairly quickly. I’ve never used it to go from almost empty to full, so I’m not sure how quickly a full charge takes. I do short bursts of about 15 minutes and it charges quickly enough to get me through another hour. I plug it in between uses to keep it full. Love the size and the multiple types of charging ports it has. Works for my earbuds (which are not Apple). Slim and compact. Easy to carry in my crossbody purse and my small clear bag for the stadium. Would buy again.

top_reviews.review_headline: Great and convenient! Slow pack charge once dead
top_reviews.review_body: It charges my iPhone 3 times before the pack dies. The chords it comes with is super duper convenient!I will say that it takes about 8 hours to fully charge from dead, so its a pretty slow charging time on the pack itself which is why it is not 5 stars.

name: MARBERO Portable Power Station 88Wh Camping Lithium Battery Solar Generator Fast Charging with AC Outlet 120W Peak Power Bank(Solar Panel Optional) for Home Backup Outdoor Emergency RV Van Hunting
description:
EFFICIENT CHARGING: Use the adapter included in the package to charge the power station from 0 to 80% in 2hrs, and use the this power supply to charge other devices at the same time while charging, which could be equivalent to a charging strip , improving the efficiency of uses. It can be charged efficiently with MARBERO's dedicated 30W or 60W solar panel, suitable for outdoor or backyard camping
COMPACT & PORTABLE DESIGN: Super slim size(6.5 x 4.6 x 3.1 inches), the portable power station is about the size of a basic DSLR; With the strong yet lightweight(3.2lbs) plastic case, plus an ergonomic handle, it's quite suitable for outdoor camping, road trips, backyard camping, home, school, office,etc.
MULTI-OUTPUT: There are 8 output ports could be used at the same time, and the built-in BMS system ensures stability and safety. The car DC charger output cable(included) will supply power to car refrigerators, vacuum cleaners, and air compressor in emergencies. USB and USB C PD ports are compatible for all the mobile phones(1hr full), tablets(1.5hrs full), laptops(2hrs full), it's an essential unit for road trips. The two AC output ports will power home appliances, such as small TVs, game consoles, projectors, inflatable beds, drones, etc.
BRIGHT LED LIGHTS: With three levels of brightness, the portable power bank can be used as a large-capacity professional flashlight when needed, which is very suitable for use when the car breaks down, power outage at home, or a tent power supply. The 1st level can be used for 68hrs(light up a small room), the 2nd level can be used for 48hrs(lighting for walking at night), and the 3rd level can be used for 37hrs(light up the living room). In case of emergency, there is SOS flashing mode, use the international SOS frequency to call for help.
SECURED & ASSURED: The Battery Management System (BMS) allows for voltage control, temperature control and more advanced safety operations. Designed with the cooling vents on the back to help this portable power supply keep the temperature constant, its internal electrical elements will not be damaged by high temperatures. When it comes to short circuit or overload, it automatically shuts off the power to protect itself and the powered devices. By using the cylindrical batteries which meets UL safety standards. UL drop tested (3 drops from 0.9m on any kind of surface).
PACKAGE INCLUDES: 1*MARBERO Portable Power Station M82,1*Power Adapter,1*Cigarette Lighter Output,1*User Manual

top_reviews.review_body: I bought this device almost a full year ago when our power went out and it worked excellent for us in need of emergency! It came early next morning after ordering it the night before. It had full power, and we were able to charge our devices and small lights till our power came back on. It charges our devices fast and still has plenty of power to charge more devices. It lasts long! And when you charge the device itself, it's incredibly fast too. I love that it has plenty of ports, has an LED flashlight, an LED battery indicator, a handle and is high in quality. This power station is so worth the cost. EXCELLENT device! I'm very happy with it!

top_reviews.review_headline: Reliable Portable Power Station for Camping & Emergencies
top_reviews.review_body: This portable power station is a must-have for outdoor trips and home backups. It charges my phone, laptop, and camping lights all at once with no issues. The battery holds a charge for weeks when not in use, and it recharges quickly via solar panels or wall outlet. The LED display is easy to read, showing remaining power and charging status clearly. It’s lightweight and compact, fitting right into my backpack. Solid build quality—definitely worth the investment.

We live in a 10 so we use us to charge our phones every single night. Only thing is is that if we plug a fan into it it only lasts an hour but anything else like smaller electronics it lasts a very long time. Super durable charges up very fast battery life stays long our top quality product. The brightness on it is great. There’s three different levels that you can use. You can use it for a light and you can also use it to charge your electronics as well.

UPDATED: Company ultimately issued me a replacement unit which solved the problems I was experiencing. Still bummed my first unit had problems but appreciate getting quality service, and conceptually love this product and the idea of having a portable laptop / cell phone battery bank.ORIGINAL: I'm a professional sound recordist and bought this (along with a solar panel by same company) to be able to recharge laptop, phone, and other small electronics while in the field. On paper it's great, I like the compact design, the flashlight is a nice feature, and it has a ton of ports. The problem is that my model seems to suffer from unpredictable, sudden battery charge loss. E.g. sometimes it behaves as expected (I've done a ton of recharge for the watt-hours of my devices and this battery, so understand what I'm asking of it) - other times the battery will either self-discharge and go from 4 bars to zero, or start recharging a cell phone and then totally fail. As a result I don't consider it trustworthy to bring into the field and unfortunately that's kind of a deal-breaker for a device like this. I'll be contacting the manufacturer to see if they'll offer a replacement battery, but I'm not sure I'll be able to trust it again which is a shame. If you're a professional and have low tolerance for this device failing, I'd purchase something else. But if it's a fun toy for recreational use, this will probably work for you.

I’m constantly on the road for work and it’s complicated finding somewhere to charge your laptop or phones. This portable power pack works great and powered my laptop and phone when I am running low on battery. I charged this overnight and it typically lasts me a couple of days. Yes I get a couple of charge off of it and I like how small it is. If it’s perfect in my backpack with my laptop.

I got this in August 2024 and it is only now January 2025 and it doesn't seem to be working correctly. I just tried to use it to power my laptop while it itself is plugged in and it was fine for a little while but all of a sudden stopped doing anything and won't power the laptop any longer. I have the Marbero battery plugged in and I pressed the Power button and then pressed the Outlet button. Like I said, it was fine, the blue battery bars were full and the red plug picture lit up. Now the blue battery bars is only showing one, it's blinking, and when I try and turn on the Outlet button it blinks red quickly but doesn't do anything. I've checked the outlet this device is plugged into and it's working fine and other things that are plugged into the same outlet are working no problem.I originally gave this a great review and I thought it was great and very helpful but it hasn't even been a year and I've only used it I think two other times (aside from when I first got it to test it out) so I'm really annoyed by this. Below is my previous review.I work from home so that means that I need to have my modem/router and laptop plugged in and getting juice for the entire time I’m working. Thankfully, my shifts are only two hours at a time and neither my router nor laptop are all that power-hungry. But any time that I’m not able to work that’s time that I’m not getting paid for and that’s obviously a problem. My power has gone out more than once while I’ve been working so I’ve been looking for something like this for a while and it happened again recently so I decided enough was enough. I got this hoping that I can prevent losing any work time (and money.)I’ve looked at other, similar power stations, but they were either way too expensive, way too big/overkill for my needs, or both. This one was inexpensive and had a coupon and it seems to be able to do what I need. I especially like that it has regular outlets for things that have two or three prongs (like my modem/router). In addition to that it’s got multiple USB ports, a USB C port (for my laptop), and even has a port where you can plug in a car charger (it also includes the car charger adapter thing). In addition to that, there is a big flashlight that is nice and bright and has three brightness levels and an SOS mode. If you do use the flashlight keep in mind that aside from that eating up power from the device, the brightness that you have it set to will determine how long you’re able to use it. In other words, if you have it at the brightest level you won’t be able to use it as long as if you have it at the dimmest level. The same thing goes for what you’re charging with it. If you’re using it to charge a phone you will get more use out of it than if you’re using it to power a computer. Different devices use different amounts of power so it’s hard to say how much of a charge you will get from what you’re using it for.The entire device is quite small, smaller than I realized when purchasing it, and is also very light. I think it would be useful to have when road-tripping, camping, backpacking, etc. because of how tiny and portable it is.I also want to point out that you can get a compatible solar panel to use with this which can help charge it and recharging it via a wall outlet will bring you up to around 80% in about two hours. Now, there are others out there that will charge to around 80% in less time, but they’re also quite a bit more money. This one also has these safety features that I wanted to point out: Short-circuit Protection, Low-voltage Protection, Over-current Protection, Over-voltage Protection, Over-load Protection. So in simple terms, you don’t need to worry about it lol.

top_reviews.review_headline: The Best Portable Charger for Every Need!
top_reviews.review_body: I’ve tried many power banks before, but INIU has quickly become my go-to brand. Their 20,000 mAh, 65 W power bank is the most powerful, compact, and reliable charger I’ve ever owned.Charging Ability: With 65 W output, it enables me to charge almost all my devices at their maximum charging speed. For my Samsung S22 Ultra, it takes about an hour to charge back to full. I mainly start charging my phone again throughout the day when it's around 30% and to get up to around 80% again with this portable charger takes about 30 minutes and only takes away around 15 to 20% of the portable charger's full capacity. I also bought this charger to help charge my Rog Ally, but I haven't tested it yet. I estimate it could charge the Rog Ally to full again pretty quickly, but use up around 70% of its power. I can confidently say though I can get multiple phone charges no problem without worrying this will run out of battery with 20,000 mAh.Size: I love the size of this mini portable charger. Even with 20,000 mAh, it’s compact enough to meet airline carry-on requirements and fits neatly into my backpack or travel pouch without feeling like dead weight. It is around half the size of my first Inui charger that I've bought, which is the normal size. I feel like this one is small enough to also slip into your pocket, no issue.Design: The build feels premium and durable. I like the easy-to-use on-screen display, which works by pressing the power button once. A big thing I like about this one in particular is the cord that is tied to the side of the device. This cord makes it so convenient to charge my phone when I don't have any wires with me on hand. The cord on the side of the device is USB-C and supports up to 100 watts.If you need one reliable charger for a phone, laptop, or travel emergencies, INIU is where it’s at. I’ve used cheaper brands, but nothing comes close to this perfect balance of speed, capacity, and portability. It's absolutely the best power bank I’ve ever owned.

Assuming this works as well as a similar product, I'm excited! Fast charging of the bank and quick charging of my iphone, I pad, and even my laptop. This device may be shorter than some others, but it is a bit thicker. Its heavy, but worth the space in my purse. The more compact version fits well in a small purse (abo it the size of a couple compacts) . This unit is convenient because it comes with a small charging cable and built in storage (although i wouldn't trust using the cable as a carry strap) Assume all is good if I don't update the review!

I already owned an INIU portable charger, and because it worked so well, I decided to purchase another one. Just like the first, this one charges my devices quickly and reliably. It’s compact, easy to carry, and holds a great charge. Very happy with the quality and performance—definitely a brand I trust for portable chargers

top_reviews.review_headline: Awesome power in a little package! Definitely worth the value. A++
top_reviews.review_body: I love the simplistic design. I got the yellow one which looks really bright and forth coming with my electronics setup. The included USB c cable is smart.. since it connects as a carry strap. The mini is a perfect size and it functions well to keep a charge on my laptop. A word of advice for someone needing to charge such a device.... The 65w charger doesn't really add to the charge of my HP 4k screen laptop while I'm using it. The fans kick in to keep the motherboard and battery cool while charging thru the dedicated USB which eats up the charge while charging. There's no way around it tho!! However it does maintain its charge for an hour or more with the battery connected.

Cons: 65 watt output is highly questionable. My laptop charger is 65watt and charges it fast while using. This charger using the correct port rated for 65watta barely charges during use. It does keep it at where it is. If it’s at 80% charge it will stay at 80% for the entire time. If I had to guess the actual output I would say 35watts. It is just enough to hold the same charge percentage the laptop was at when plugged in for 4-5hours. I have tried 240 watt rated USB C cables it does seem to charge a hair faster and with this will slowly charge. It is still no where near as fast as the 65watt wall charger that comes with the laptop.Pros: even though it does seem to have enough output to charge it more while using it is just enough to not lose any laptop battery and continue to use the laptop for almost 5 hours! Which is extremely impressive my laptop alone maybe gets 2.5 hours. So this adds about 4.5-5hrs to that giving up to 7 hours of constant use which is awesome.To sum it up this charger with is capacity was almost had the price of Anker and I green. It works great for the price . I am confident Anker and Ugreen do charge at the advertised 65watt. I am confident this one does not do that. It is still fast enough to work fine with you laptop I suggest plugging it in when your laptop is at 80% or higher. It will hold that level for the entire time you use it.

top_reviews.review_headline: Awesome charger, perfect weight and size with a lot of power
top_reviews.review_body: I really like this power bank it is well designed and the perfect size to power up for several days. It has a crisp display that tells the battery percentage digitally. I got the yellow one and I really like the color. I like how the battery charges up fast and charges at 45 W. If you’re a fan of Iniu you should add this to your collection and also get the 25,000 mah battery as well. The attached cable is also such a great idea. Which I prefer a longer cable so I just carry around a couple of 2 foot charging cables and it works out just fine.

top_reviews.review_headline: Works well, holds a good charge
top_reviews.review_body: Charges a lot of things before it runs out, takes a while to recharge but nothing abnormal.
